context: 5-panel adversarial stress test of the product-first workflow refactor on https://solar-archive.onrender.com. Five agents tested orthogonal slices (cold load + product picker, variant + transition, image picker + master toggle, editor + buy, cross-step nav). Findings consolidated here. Verified-fixed items live in git history (commits f7d6312 through 6b474de); only OPEN concerns appear below.
verified_fixed_in_6b474de: Master toggle initial active state matches cards (Original=raw)
Master toggle stays position:fixed after back-from-editor
Browser back from editor is single-step (no longer a no-op)
left-rail-preview body class clears on non-editor transitions
Breadcrumb and master toggle stack vertically without overlap
Confirm-select modal shows placeholder when no source image

open_items.title: Quality timeline Raw + RHEF stuck on 'Loading…' indefinitely after a vibe selection
open_items.description: When user picks a famous-moment vibe card (e.g. AR 2192), the editor opens with the Helioviewer JPG as state.originalImage. The Quality timeline's Raw and RHEF steps remain 'Loading…' for 30+ seconds and never auto-advance. Clicking the Raw radio fires a DOM-level state change but the canvas does not switch and JPG stays the active filter.
open_items.root_cause_hypothesis: Vibe-card flow uses pre-warmed manifest URLs for Raw + RHEF (/asset/default/vibe/<slug>/raw_full.png etc.) but those URLs are not preloaded into state.rawImage / state.rhefImage. The filter timeline's _filterIsReady() check reads those state fields, so it sees nothing ready beyond JPG. Need a path that pulls the manifest URLs into state.{raw,rhef}Image when a vibe is activated.
repro: Cold load → Pick a variant (any product) → Continue
Observe Quality timeline Raw + RHEF stuck on 'Loading…'
open_items.fix_sketch: In _activateVibe (or commitImageChoice when state.activeVibeSlug is set), pre-load the manifest's raw_full_url and rhef_full_url into state.rawImage / state.rhefImage via Image() objects. Then call maybeAutoAdvanceFilter to let the timeline catch up.
open_items.estimated_effort: medium (~2-3h: state plumbing + cache coordination + visual verify)

open_items.title: Tablet ~768px viewport loses the entire editor toolbar
open_items.description: At width=768, the right column with Quality timeline, Geometry/Adjust/Tools tabs, sliders, and Change-product/wavelength/variant buttons is completely missing. User sees only the preview canvas, 'Generate real mockup' and 'Download your design' buttons, then footer. Editing is impossible on tablet. A black ~150px band appears on the right edge of viewport (stale rail-mode gutter).
open_items.root_cause_hypothesis: The rail-mode CSS engages above a breakpoint (_LEFT_RAIL_BREAKPOINT in solar-archive.js, possibly 720 or 768) and reserves a gutter for the rail preview. At 768 the gutter shows but the editor-main column doesn't fit, gets hidden / overflowed.
repro: Cold load → Pick a variant → Continue → click any vibe
Observe editor section: only canvas + bottom CTA buttons visible, no toolbar/tabs/sliders. Black gutter on right edge.
code_pointers: api/solar-archive.css search for 'left-rail-preview' and 'single-preview-mode' media query rules
open_items.fix_sketch: Either (a) bump _LEFT_RAIL_BREAKPOINT to 1024 so rail engages only on desktop, OR (b) make the rail's CSS-vars resolver collapse the editor-main column gracefully at narrow widths.

open_items.title: Cold-load with `#image` hash strands user in step-image without a selected product
open_items.description: Cold-loading https://solar-archive.onrender.com/#image enters body.step-image, vibe grid is visible, no state.selectedProduct. User picks a vibe and downstream editor lacks a product. Similar issue after Chrome BFCache restore: the line-198 demote safety net runs on cold init but not on pageshow restore.
open_items.root_cause_hypothesis: The init guard at solar-archive.js ~line 200 demotes when (currentStep != product && !selectedProduct), but only runs on first DOMContentLoaded. BFCache restore + the hash-route logic don't re-run that guard.
repro: Observe body.step-image, no selectedProduct, vibe grid visible
Pick AR 2192 → editor opens with no product context
code_pointers: Add a pageshow event listener that re-runs the demote check
open_items.fix_sketch: Wrap the demote check in a function (`_normalizeStepWithState()`) and call it from both the bootstrap path AND a pageshow event listener. Also call it before each setStep() call in commitProductChoice / commitImageChoice.

open_items.title: Floating 'Request new item' + 'Leave a comment' pills occlude product card content
open_items.description: On both desktop (1400) and mobile widths, the two feedback FAB pills sit fixed bottom-right and visually cover the third column's title / 'From $XX' / 'Pick a variant' button on the top product row at desktop, and overlap card text on mobile.
code_pointers: Current rule shifts FAB up only when body.left-rail-preview; need step-product shift too
open_items.fix_sketch: Add body.step-product .feedback-fab-group { bottom: 88px; right: 18px; } so the pills clear the bottom row of the product grid. Already have the body.left-rail-preview shift; mirror it for step-product.

open_items.title: First product card is not above the fold on mobile (~390px)
open_items.description: At rendered viewport 390x667, firstCard.top = 380px of 667px viewport. Hero + tagline + courtesy line + beta banner + section header consume the top ~360px. Only the product image is visible; no price/CTA. Defeats the point of product-first.
open_items.fix_sketch: On mobile (max-width: 600px), shrink hero (collapse the courtesy line, smaller H1, smaller beta banner) or move the hero to a collapsed accordion. Net target: first product card visible at y < 120px.

open_items.title: Rapid toggle clicks leave multiple is-pending-target pills (visual transient)
open_items.description: Spamming Preview → Original → Filtered → Preview → Original at ~200ms cadence produced a moment where both jpg and raw pills carried is-pending-target while rhef still held is-active. After ~5s state settled correctly on last-clicked tier with cards in sync. No long-term oscillation, but the in-flight visual is muddled.
open_items.fix_sketch: In the master-toggle click handler, before adding is-pending-target to the new clicked button, REMOVE is-pending-target from all other buttons. Currently the race-guard handles the END state but not the in-flight chain.

open_items.title: Acrylic Print's 'Pick a variant' button sometimes skips variant selection
open_items.description: Clicking 'Pick a variant' on the Acrylic Wall Art card jumps straight to step-image with no .variant-panel ever opening (the inner panel stays empty/hidden). Variant choice is silently bypassed. Could not reproduce in subsequent agent runs — possibly a race or a Chrome extension stealing the click.
open_items.fix_sketch: Add console logging in showConfirmSelectModal entry + bail paths so next agent run can pinpoint where the click is being lost. Also harden the click handler to guarantee the modal opens before any step transition fires.

open_items.title: Customize section section-collapsed class doesn't toggle correctly on click
open_items.description: After clicking the configSection header, #configSection carries class 'section-collapsed' but content (HEK tiles + 9-wavelength grid) is visible. Manually removing the class via DOM confirms the content renders correctly. So the click adds the wrong class state.
open_items.root_cause_hypothesis: _wireConfigSectionToggle uses classList.toggle('section-collapsed') which returns true when class is NOW present. The handler then sets aria-expanded based on a misnamed variable. Possibly the initial HTML class state + the JS toggle interact incorrectly when the user lands on step-image (where the section is fresh and the HTML class is 'section-collapsed').
open_items.fix_sketch: Replace the misleading toggle pattern with explicit add/remove + a boolean derived from the CURRENT state BEFORE the toggle. e.g. `var wasCollapsed = section.classList.contains('section-collapsed'); section.classList.toggle('section-collapsed', !wasCollapsed); toggle.setAttribute('aria-expanded', wasCollapsed ? 'true' : 'false');`

open_items.title: Scroll position not reset on step-product → step-image transition
open_items.description: If the user scrolled before clicking 'Pick a variant', the post-transition scrollY is preserved (or clamped if doc shrinks). User lands mid-page or near the bottom of the vibe grid rather than at the 'Pick an image — a famous moment…' header.
open_items.fix_sketch: In commitProductChoice, after setStep('image'), call window.scrollTo({top: 0, behavior: 'auto'}) or _scrollToEl(.vibe-grid-section, 'start') so the user always starts at the top of the new step's content.

open_items.title: 'Behind the scenes while your image renders' modal pops on every editor entry
open_items.description: Until user checks 'Don't show again', the data-credits modal auto-pops on every cold vibe-card → editor flow. The dismiss-permanently checkbox works, but the friction is the FIRST instance: it covers the canvas + tools without warning.
open_items.fix_sketch: Either (a) remove the auto-pop entirely and keep only the footer 'Data credits' link as the discoverable surface, OR (b) delay the pop to 5+ seconds AFTER editor mount so the user has seen their image first, AND make the modal smaller/sidebar-style instead of center-stage. User preference required.

open_items.title: body.single-preview-mode lingers after back-from-editor
open_items.description: After leaving the editor, body class single-preview-mode is not cleared (left-rail-preview IS cleared by the new _applyStep logic). Not user-visible because #selectedProductPreview is display:none via step CSS, but the stale class smells and could affect any future rule that selects on single-preview-mode.
open_items.fix_sketch: Add single-preview-mode to the body.classList.remove() list in _applyStep when name !== 'editor'.

open_items.title: Vibe-card click fires 'Full-resolution RHEF ready!' instead of '193 Å loaded!'
open_items.description: Wavelength-tile clicks correctly show '171 Å loaded!'. Vibe-card clicks fire 'Full-resolution RHEF ready!' because the manifest's HQ RHEF lands quickly and the HQ-ready toast pre-empts the per-wavelength one. Cosmetic ordering issue.
open_items.fix_sketch: Suppress the HQ-ready toast for the first 1500ms after image load OR show '193 Å loaded!' synchronously before any tier-promotion toasts fire.

open_items.title: Locked tier radios are click-able but no-op
open_items.description: Quality-timeline radios have label 'LOCKED' but the underlying <input> has disabled=false. A user click silently no-ops with no tooltip or toast feedback.
open_items.fix_sketch: Either (a) set disabled=true on the input when the step status is 'locked', OR (b) wire a click handler that shows a toast ('HQ RHEF rendering in background — try again in ~30s'). (b) is friendlier but more work.

open_items.title: Bogus hash like #abc123 occasionally not cleared on fallback
open_items.description: Cold-load #abc123 correctly falls back to body.step-product but location.hash is sometimes left as #abc123 rather than cleared. The history.replaceState path may race with a subsequent _applyStep call.
open_items.fix_sketch: Re-check that the hash-clear replaceState happens AFTER any subsequent _applyStep that might write its own hash. Wrap in a single deferred microtask.

open_items.title: /asset/hq_SDO_193_20141024.png returns 404
open_items.description: Network shows a 404 fetched during page load for the legacy default-HQ asset path. Cosmetic — the manifest-based path supersedes this — but a stale reference exists somewhere in the code.
code_pointers: Search for 'hq_SDO_193_20141024' in api/ to find the fetcher
open_items.fix_sketch: Update the URL to a valid asset path OR delete the fetcher if no longer needed.

open_items.title: T-shirt 2-axis variant modal hides scrollable variant tile list
open_items.description: On 2-axis products (color × size), the modal renders only color swatches + size chips. Per-variant tile list with prices is suppressed by _twoAxis mode. Users can't see per-variant price diffs at a glance.
open_items.fix_sketch: In _twoAxis mode, optionally render a compact 'matrix' view (color rows × size columns) with prices in cells, OR show the full tile list below the chips. UX decision: do users WANT to compare prices per cell, or is the color+size chip selector enough?

open_items.title: Mixed straight quotes and unicode primes in Framed Poster sizes
open_items.description: Variants like 8" x 11", 11″ x 14″, 12″ x 18″ — mixed quote characters in the same list. Comes from Printify catalog.
open_items.fix_sketch: Normalize at the API layer (/api/printify/variants) — replace all unicode primes with straight quotes in variant size labels.

open_items.title: No 'Buy' / 'Create on Shopify' button visible in the editor
open_items.description: The task brief referenced a Buy button gated on HQ RHEF with a progress indicator. Neither exists in the live editor. Only 'Generate real mockup' (enabled) and 'Download your design' (disabled until mockup) are present. Either this is BETA_MODE gating (Buy hidden when BETA is on) or an unshipped feature.
open_items.fix_sketch: Check BETA_MODE flag state on prod. If BETA is on, this is by design and the docs should reflect. If not, surface the Buy button.

open_items.title: Acrylic Wall Art 8x8 size tile missing Horizontal/Vertical orientation label
open_items.description: Sizes 7x5, 5x7, 10x8, 8x10, 11x11 all carry an orientation label, but 8x8 (square) does not. Inconsistent layout makes the 8x8 tile look truncated.
open_items.fix_sketch: Either always show the orientation label (use 'Square' for 8x8 / 11x11), or strip it from all tiles when the product has any square variant. Decide on copy.

out_of_scope_or_test_artifacts.title: Render cold-start 502
out_of_scope_or_test_artifacts.note: Free-tier hibernation. Not fixable from the app.

out_of_scope_or_test_artifacts.title: ?clean=1 / ?fresh=mobile / ?cb=q1 query param mutations during testing
out_of_scope_or_test_artifacts.note: Confirmed NOT in our codebase (grep -r returned 0 matches). These come from the test agent's Playwright scaffolding (cache-bust query params) or from a Chrome extension. Disregard.

out_of_scope_or_test_artifacts.title: Multi-color chooser auto-commits vs confirm-select two-step
out_of_scope_or_test_artifacts.note: Intentional UX: color chooser is single-axis (one tap = commit), confirm-select is multi-axis (color + size). Bifurcation is by design.

out_of_scope_or_test_artifacts.title: Confirm-modal backdrop element with display:block in DOM after modal close
out_of_scope_or_test_artifacts.note: False alarm: parent .confirm-modal.hidden has display:none, so the child element's rect collapses to zero. Visually hidden. Agent misread the computed style.

out_of_scope_or_test_artifacts.title: Step badge reported as '2' on cold load by one agent
out_of_scope_or_test_artifacts.note: False alarm: prod has step-badge=1 on productSection (verified via curl). Agent hit a stale cache.

priority_recommendation: STRESS-001 (editor filter timeline stuck on Loading) — biggest UX gap
STRESS-002 (tablet 768px loses toolbar) — significant viewport blocked
STRESS-005 (mobile fold) — defeats product-first if user is on phone
STRESS-004 (FAB occludes product cards) — quick win, CSS-only
